theme: glass furnace product wheel across demand streams
title: Glass Furnace Product Wheel: Dilution-Routed Changeovers
strategies: naive fixed-order wheel: visit every colour once in the given input order, size each lot to a generic demand share of a guessed cycle length (ignores routing and true cycle time entirely)

fixed-matrix nearest-neighbour recipe: compute the direct dilution changeover cost for every ordered colour pair once, build a single-visit wheel greedily by nearest-neighbour on that matrix, size lots with a self-consistent EOQ-style fixed point -- never questions whether a colour should be produced more than once

shortest-path-informed local search: compute the all-pairs shortest path over the direct dilution-cost graph (Floyd-Warshall) to expose non-additive shortcuts, seed several nearest-neighbour tours on that effective graph, refine with 2-opt and single-colour relocation scored on the TRUE lot-sized total-cost objective, then selectively expand tour edges into their real shortest paths (inserting genuine extra campaigns for catalyst colours) only when it demonstrably lowers total cost

stronger metaheuristics: simulated annealing / large-neighbourhood search over the joint (order, repeat-count, lot-size) space, or an exact small-K dynamic program over subsets with an explicit revisit budget
